title: Russian Intelligence Services Target Commercial Messaging Application Accounts
summary: CISA and the Federal Bureau of Investigation released a Public Service Announcement (PSA) warning about ongoing phishing campaigns by cyber actors associated with the Russian Intelligence Services targeting commercial messaging applications (CMAs). These campaigns aim to bypass encryption to compromise to individual user accounts with targets including current and former U.S. government...

sections.context: This reflects continued use of phishing as an initial-access path in broader intrusion chains.

title: CISA Adds Five Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
summary: CISA has added five new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation.
email_summary: CISA has added five new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation.

title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
summary: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets named Aisuru , Kimwolf , JackSkid and Mossad are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed...

sections.context: This fits the broader data-exposure trend where follow-on abuse of leaked information is common.
